commit.message:
docs: Web向け指示を現行コマンドへ更新する

.github/instructions の apps/web 向け指示を task verify 前提へ更新。
旧 task check / task test 前提の案内を現行の検証フローに合わせて修正。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(refill): advance queue to OPE-236 batch

Summary:
- record OPE-233 and OPE-234 as completed in the refill history
- move OPE-236 and OPE-237 into the active batch
- leave OPE-238 as the next standby slice

Rationale:
- keep the repo-native refill state aligned with the live Symphony and
  Linear execution state
- preserve a current handoff record on `main` while the active issue
  branches continue in parallel

Tests:
- not run (queue metadata only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(refill): promote OPE-237 into active batch

Summary:
- move OPE-237 into the active refill set to keep three parallel-safe
  slices running
- reorder the next standby batch so OPE-236 waits behind the current
  distributed API work and avoids avoidable file overlap
- mark OPE-235 as completed in the queue history

Rationale:
- preserve parallel throughput while reducing merge-conflict pressure on
  `bigclaw-go/internal/api/distributed.go`
- keep the repo-native refill order aligned with the live Linear state
  and the actual safe execution sequence

Tests:
- not run (queue metadata only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:

- keep the repo-native refill source aligned with the newly created

commit.message:
refactor: WebのTaskfile順を整理する

apps/web の Taskfile で日常利用時に追いやすい順へタスク定義を並び替え。
format 系を隣接させ、開発系と検証系の見通しを改善。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(refill): seed OPE-233 batch and SSH sync

Summary:
- switch Symphony workspace bootstrap to SSH clone plus `gh auth
  setup-git` so new issue workspaces no longer depend on
  `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- advance the parallel refill queue/docs/tests to the new
  OPE-233/OPE-234/OPE-235 batch
- keep the recorded active/standby slices aligned with the new
  Linear backlog created for the next distributed wave

Rationale:
- ensure unattended Symphony runs can keep creating GitHub-backed
  workspaces in the current local environment
- make the refill source of truth match the newly opened parallel
  issues so automatic promotion and dashboard state stay coherent

Tests:
- `./elixir/bin/symphony --help`
- `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:4000/api/v1/state`
- `python3 -m pytest tests/test_parallel_refill.py -q` (exit 139 in
  this environment)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore: Web検証タスクを整理する

Web の Taskfile に typecheck と format を追加し、verify で包括検証を並列実行できるよう更新。
AGENTS.md と README の検証手順も新しいタスク構成へ同期。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): describe review and PR body flow

Summary:
- document the simplified machine-filled PR template shape
- document that Rework runs consume blocking GitHub review feedback directly
- document that Linear now stores only a short review-status breadcrumb

Rationale:
- the runtime and workflow behavior changed, and README should describe the shipped defaults accurately
- this avoids confusion when PR bodies look intentionally minimal and review details stay on GitHub

Tests:
- npx prettier --check README.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(harness): record final gate evidence for cli cleanup

Summary:
- add a final gate summary for the accepted CLI contract cleanup branch head
- record the passing GitHub-backed CI export and retained final evidence bundle
- refresh the publish record with the current published head SHA

Rationale:
- the archived plan should capture the final gate outcome in the repository
- landing should proceed from a branch whose tracked records match the accepted
  gate evidence

Tests:
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/export_ci_status.sh main --docs-updated true --pr 44
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/final_gate.sh .local/loop/review-20260315-151235.json .local/loop/ci-status-8aebeeac2821.json docs/harness/completed/2026-03-15-cli-dev-contract-cleanup.md main
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(refill): advance queue to OPE-230 batch

Summary:
- mark OPE-228 and OPE-229 as completed in the repo refill queue
- promote OPE-230 and OPE-231 as the active parallel batch with OPE-232
  queued as standby
- refresh the parallel refill assertions to match the new Linear order

Rationale:
- keeping the repo queue aligned with Linear preserves the manual and
  Symphony-compatible refill workflow between coding turns

Tests:

commit.message:
fix: レビュー指摘の認証ストーリーと監視送信を修正

AuthPage の story decorator の重複を解消し、Sentry へ認証エラーの description を送らないように修正。
あわせて Sentry 送信内容を固定するテストを追加。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api): surface checkpoint reset review summary

Summary:
- expose recent checkpoint reset summaries in debug status and
  control-center audit payloads
- add global recent-reset querying to SQLite and HTTP-backed event-log
  implementations plus service endpoints
- cover the new review surface with API and HTTP event-log regression
  tests

Rationale:
- operators need a control-plane view of recent reset activity without
  drilling directly into the checkpoint store
- surfacing reset summaries keeps distributed review flows aligned with
  the new durable reset audit trail

Tests:
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/events ./internal/api
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(codex): match PR body renderer to template

Summary:
- update PR body rendering to fill only the current template sections
  for Linear Issue, Context, and Summary
- remove stale assumptions about TL;DR, Alternatives, and Test Plan
- align app-server tests with the simplified PR template

Rationale:
- the old renderer only replaced sections that no longer exist in the
  shipped template, leaving hidden HTML comment placeholders behind
- GitHub hides those comment placeholders, which made generated PRs
  look empty apart from headings

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/codex/app-server.test.ts
- npm run build
- npx prettier --check src/codex/app-server.ts test/codex/app-server.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(harness): record real subagent review rerun

Summary:
- record the successful full-pr reviewer-subagent rerun after the Codex reset
- mark the accepted final review evidence as round 20260315-151235
- note the passing aggregate contract status alongside the earlier fallback round

Rationale:
- the archived harness record should reflect the true accepted review evidence
- the repository should not leave manual-fallback review notes as the last word
  once real reviewer artifacts have been produced

Tests:
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_finalize.sh 20260315-151235 .local/loop/review-20260315-151235-*.json
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(events): add checkpoint reset audit history

Summary:
- persist checkpoint reset audit snapshots in SQLite and expose them
  through event-log service, API, and HTTP client history endpoints
- include reset audit payloads in delete checkpoint responses and cover
  reset history survival after replay resumes
- advance the parallel refill queue to OPE-228/OPE-229/OPE-230 and
  refresh distributed reliability docs

Rationale:
- operators need a durable record of checkpoint resets once retained
  replay boundaries force manual recovery
- keeping the refill queue aligned with Linear preserves unattended
  Symphony-compatible parallel execution while the local CLI is blocked

Tests:
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/events ./internal/api
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./...
- cd BigClaw && PYTHONPATH=src python3 - <<'PY' ... refill checks

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(events): add expired checkpoint diagnostics and reset

Summary:
- surface durable checkpoint expiry diagnostics through event replay and
  stream checkpoint APIs
- add checkpoint reset support across SQLite and HTTP-backed event-log
  stores so expired subscribers can recover safely
- document the fail-closed replay reset flow and cover it with API and
  event-log regression tests

Rationale:
- durable replay should not silently reinterpret aged-out checkpoints
  once retention boundaries have moved past them
- operators need an explicit reset workflow that preserves the replay
  boundary evidence while allowing recovery from the earliest retained
  event

Tests:
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/events ./internal/api
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
test(cli): cover activated wrapper fetch smoke

Summary:
- add an activation-contract regression that runs `missless fetch`
  through the repo-local wrapper on the supported sourced entrypoint
- keep the archived harness plan aligned with the final branch state
  after the review-loop follow-up

Rationale:
- the review loop surfaced that wrapper coverage stopped at `--help`
  and `print-draft-contract`, which left real subcommand forwarding
  under the supported developer entrypoint untested
- recording the extra smoke in the completed plan keeps repository
  history consistent with the shipped branch state and PR narrative

Tests:
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/activation-cli.test.ts
- pnpm -r test
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(skills): clarify activation-based missless usage

Summary:
- update product skill authoring guidance to show the current repo-local
  activation flow for `missless`
- make the supported command exposure explicit during the current development
  phase

Rationale:
- skill authors should not infer a packaged or globally installed runtime while
  the repository only supports session-local activation
- the shared skill guidance should stay aligned with the canonical CLI contract

Tests:
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/activation-cli.test.ts
- rg -n "stable installable runtime command|installable runtime" skills/README.md
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(harness): record publish outcome for CLI contract cleanup

Summary:
- extend the archived harness plan with the final review-loop result details
- record the created PR and issue sync outcome for the CLI contract cleanup

Rationale:
- the archived plan should reflect the actual review and publish steps that ran
- keeping the repository record current avoids splitting execution history
  between chat state and tracked docs

Tests:
- not run (documentation-only publish record update)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(harness): record cli contract review outcome

Summary:
- record the passing full-pr review result for the CLI dev contract cleanup
- capture the bash coverage follow-up, skill-guidance alignment, and issue #42
  sync in the archived plan

Rationale:
- the archived harness record should reflect the actual review-loop outcome used
  before publish
- keeping the issue sync link and review notes in the plan makes the branch
  evidence self-contained

Tests:
- git diff --check
- rg -n "stable installable runtime command|installable runtime" skills/README.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(skills): align skill guidance with local activation contract

Summary:
- update the shared skills README to describe stable runtime command names
- document repo-local activation as the supported way to expose those commands

Rationale:
- the current CLI contract no longer promises an installed packaged runtime
- shared skill guidance should stay aligned with the repository README and
  skill-specific instructions

Tests:
- not run (docs-only follow-up)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(harness): add session-local missless activation workflow

Summary:
- add a sourced, session-local `missless` activation helper and repo-local
  wrapper for local CLI development
- rewrite the README install section around the local development flow and
  remove the old broken packaged-install commands
- archive the harness execution record and link follow-up issue #42 for the
  separate packaged-tarball blocker

Rationale:
- local development needed a rerunnable workflow that does not mutate one
  machine-global `missless` link and can safely support multiple worktrees
- the README should stay user-facing while blocker evidence lives in tracked
  execution history and issue follow-up instead of inline caveat prose

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/dev-activate-missless.sh
- bash -n scripts/bin/missless
- cd apps/cli && pnpm exec node scripts/build.mjs
- zsh -lc 'source /Users/yaozhang/.codex/worktrees/50ee/missless/scripts/dev-activate-missless.sh >/dev/null && [ "$(command -v missless)" = "/Users/yaozhang/.codex/worktrees/50ee/missless/scripts/bin/missless" ] && missless --help >/dev/null'
- zsh -lc 'alias missless="echo nope"; if source /Users/yaozhang/.codex/worktrees/50ee/missless/scripts/dev-activate-missless.sh >/tmp/missless-alias.out 2>/tmp/missless-alias.err; then exit 1; fi; grep -q "defines it as alias" /tmp/missless-alias.err'
- rg -n "source|MISSLESS_FORCE_INSTALL|current working directory" README.md
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): restore prompt template parsing

Summary:
- replace the invalid Liquid elseif tag in WORKFLOW.md with elsif
- add a repository test that renders the default workflow prompt with GitHub review feedback context

Rationale:
- the broken tag caused runtime prompt parsing to fail before agent turns started
- render-level coverage prevents syntax regressions that string-only checks miss

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/workflow.test.ts
- npm run build
- npx prettier --check WORKFLOW.md test/repository/workflow.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(review): tighten review workflow and rework context

Summary:
- add reviewer Linear issue summary, scope constraints, and a stricter
  GitHub review template
- keep only a short Linear review breadcrumb and cap automated blocking
  review loops at three rounds
- fetch latest blocking GitHub review feedback into Rework agent prompts
  and align workflow/tests with the updated PR template

Rationale:
- keep detailed review findings in GitHub while preserving enough Linear
  state for humans and automation
- stop the reviewer from judging ticket legitimacy instead of reviewing
  implementation correctness
- make Rework retries use the latest blocking review context directly
  instead of duplicated tracker comments

Tests:
- ./scripts/verify

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: preserve raw remote fleet files during collect (#69)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(events): bootstrap durable retention and broker readiness

Summary:
- add SQLite retention bootstrap support with persisted truncation
  boundaries and richer replay watermark payloads
- wire runtime bootstrap to use SQLite retention settings and expose
  broker adapter readiness through the durability plan surface
- extend event, HTTP, and durability tests plus update repo-native
  reliability and rollout reports for the new contracts

Rationale:
- make replay horizons survive process restarts for durable local
  backends so operators can reason about retained history safely
- expose broker adapter readiness in control-plane payloads before a
  live broker implementation lands, keeping the rollout contract
  implementation-facing and verifiable

Tests:
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/events ./internal/config ./cmd/bigclawd
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/api
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

- rewrite the README install section around the local development flow and
  remove the old broken packaged-install commands
- archive the harness execution record and link follow-up issue #42 for the

Rationale:
- local development needed a rerunnable workflow that does not mutate one

- zsh -lc 'alias missless="echo nope"; if source /Users/yaozhang/.codex/worktrees/50ee/missless/scripts/dev-activate-missless.sh >/tmp/missless-alias.out 2>/tmp/missless-alias.err; then exit 1; fi; grep -q "defines it as alias" /tmp/missless-alias.err'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(tracker): use documented Linear issue lookup

Summary:
- switch issue-identifier lookup to the documented issue(id: ...) query
- include GraphQL error details in 400 tracker failures
- update tracker tests for the new lookup and diagnostics

Rationale:
- the previous issues(filter: { identifier: ... }) query shape was not
  grounded in the documented Linear API and failed in review sync
- surfacing GraphQL validation details makes future schema mismatches
  actionable in GitHub Actions logs

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/tracker/linear-client.test.ts test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts
- npm run build
- npx prettier --check src/tracker/linear-client.ts test/tracker/linear-client.test.ts test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(review): install deps before sync build

Summary:
- run ./scripts/setup before building the review-sync helper
- lock the setup/build/run sequence in the repository tooling guard

Rationale:
- the review workflow was building in a fresh checkout without
  installed npm dependencies
- the sync build needs the repo's TypeScript type dependencies to
  compile before running the script

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(review): harden Linear review sync

Summary:
- move review-sync Linear calls onto the tracker client
- make projectSlug optional except for project-scoped issue queries
- build the review-sync runtime in workflow and expand test coverage

Rationale:
- the standalone sync script was using raw Linear GraphQL with opaque
  HTTP 400 failures
- the review-sync path should not invent a fake project slug for
  client methods that do not use project-scoped issue listing

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/tracker/linear-client.test.ts test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npm run build
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml src/tracker/linear-client.ts scripts/github-review-sync.mjs test/tracker/linear-client.test.ts test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(review): sync Linear on blocking review state

Summary:
- run Linear sync after a successful reviewer execution
- detect blocking feedback from CHANGES_REQUESTED reviews
- expose blocking_review workflow output and guard it with tests

Rationale:
- reviewer step outcome reports action execution success, not the
  posted review verdict
- posted changes-requested reviews should still sync to Linear and
  fail the workflow

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml scripts/github-review-sync.mjs test/scripts/github-review-sync.test.ts test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Add compact split reordering controls (#31)

* feat(split-builder): add compact reorder controls

Summary:
- add move earlier and move later controls to each saved split row
- preserve selected split editing state while rows are reordered
- tighten split-row spacing so four row actions still fit the compact
  mobile layout
- cover reorder behavior and selection preservation in main tests

Rationale:
- saved splits could be edited, duplicated, and deleted but not reordered
  while assembling a workout
- reorder actions need to work even when an input is focused, so row
  controls now activate before blur-driven rerenders can swallow the
  interaction
- the row layout stays dense instead of wrapping or overflowing at the
  mobile target width

Tests:
- npm run check
- built preview verified in Chromium with desktop and mobile split-row
  screenshots plus a 360px overflow check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(pr): refresh HEL-38 draft

Summary:
- replace the stale PR title and body draft with HEL-38 scope
- record the current validation and preview evidence for the reorder
  controls change

Rationale:
- the repo publish script reads the committed PR draft files directly, so
  stale metadata would create the wrong review artifact
- keeping the PR draft aligned with the branch scope avoids publishing a
  misleading title or checklist

Tests:
- not run (metadata-only update)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(events): expose retention watermark surfaces

Summary:
- add retention watermark types and provider hooks for memory, SQLite,
  and HTTP-backed event logs
- expose retention watermark data through `/events`, `/debug/status`,
  `/metrics`, and the event-log service surface
- refresh reliability and parallel refill docs for OPE-220 and add
  coverage for SQLite, HTTP, and API payloads

Rationale:
- make replay horizon visibility explicit so operators can reason about
  retained history before relying on after-cursor recovery
- keep the contract consistent across local and remote event-log
  backends while preserving the existing replay-safe event model

Tests:
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./internal/events ./internal/api
- cd BigClaw/bigclaw-go && go test ./...

commit.message:
fix(review): provide explicit PR diff context

Summary:
- fetch full history for the reviewed commit and capture the PR base branch
  and base SHA from the GitHub API
- generate changed-files and diff-stat artifacts for Claude and instruct it
  to use them as the source of truth for PR scope
- lock the new review-context contract in the repository tooling test

Rationale:
- Claude produced a false large-diff review because the workflow only gave
  it the checked-out repo snapshot and PR metadata, not the actual base-vs-head
  diff context
- providing explicit diff artifacts should keep review scope tied to the real
  PR changes instead of the whole repository contents at HEAD

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): ship agent-first workflow contracts (#41)

* feat(cli): ship agent-first workflow contracts

Summary:
- rename the first-slice workflow commands to fetch, validate,
  anchor, and review, and make their default stdout contract one
  JSON object with shared agent-friendly fields
- add explicit fetch-method selection and reshape source.json into a
  durable requested/decision_basis provenance artifact
- add the CLI contract spec and align skills, e2e scripts, and tests
  with the new command names and structured workflow outputs

Rationale:
- close issue #29 around durable decision-basis provenance without
  carrying provider-attempt metadata into long-lived artifacts
- make missless easier for agents and reviewers to consume by giving
  the workflow a short verb-first surface and stable machine-readable
  output envelopes
- lock the contract down with docs, installable-bin coverage, and a
  full local review loop before publish

Tests:
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(runtime): close isolated review findings

Summary:
- harden fetch provenance so explicit fetch-method requests must match
  durable provider results, and require custom providers to report durable
  chosen fetch methods
- separate fetch stdout from stderr in the e2e review wrapper and cover the
  stderr-noise path in CLI integration tests
- archive the reviewer-scope harness hardening work and record the passing
  isolated full-pr review round in the completed execution history

Rationale:
- isolated subagent review found remaining provenance and wrapper
  reliability gaps after the first PR-ready pass
- the review-loop harness needed a tracked completed record because the
  scope-enforcement change belongs in harness history, not only product docs
- the branch now needs durable evidence that isolated review was rerun and
  cleared before publish/final-gate

Tests:
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/unit/core/fetch-normalize.test.ts
- pnpm exec tsx --test tests/integration/cli/e2e-driver.test.ts
- pnpm -r build
- pnpm -r typecheck
- pnpm -r test
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_regression.sh
- .agents/skills/loop-review-loop/scripts/review_finalize.sh 20260315-092955 .local/loop/review-20260315-092955-*.json

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(plan): record final gate prereq block

Summary:
- record the attempted final-gate export command in the completed execution
  plan
- capture that final gate is currently no-go because the branch has not been
  published to an open PR yet

Rationale:
- final gate should leave a tracked repository record, even when it fails
  closed on missing publish or CI evidence
- keeping the blocker explicit avoids rerunning the same gate probe without
  first completing loop-publish

Tests:
- git diff --check
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/export_ci_status.sh main --docs-updated true

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(plan): record publish and final gate pass

Summary:
- update the completed execution record with the published PR URL and issue
  sync comment
- record the passing GitHub-backed final gate run and retained local evidence
  bundle

Rationale:
- the archived plan should reflect the real published branch state rather than
  stopping at local PR-ready status
- final gate evidence is only useful long-term if the repository history says
  which PR head and CI artifact actually passed

Tests:
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/export_ci_status.sh main --docs-updated true --pr 41
- .agents/skills/loop-final-gate/scripts/final_gate.sh .local/loop/review-20260315-092955.json .local/loop/ci-status-0e1fbe998231.json docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-03-14-cli-provenance-and-agent-output.md main
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(review): allow direct gh pr review posting

Summary:
- allow only `gh pr review` through Claude's Bash tool permissions in the
  GitHub review workflow
- instruct Claude to post reviews with a single direct command and use
  `--body-file` instead of shell composition for long messages
- lock the narrower review-posting contract in the repository tooling test

Rationale:
- review runs were completing analysis but stopping on permission denials
  when Claude tried to post via `gh pr review`
- allowing only the final review command keeps metadata discovery blocked
  while unblocking the actual review submission step

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(review): pass PR metadata into Claude workflow

Summary:
- fetch PR title, body, branch, and author from the GitHub API in the
  workflow_run review job
- inject that metadata into the Claude review prompt and forbid shell
  rediscovery of PR context
- lock the stronger prompt contract in the repository tooling guard test

Rationale:
- Claude was looping on denied gh/bash metadata lookups because the
  workflow prompt only included partial PR context
- providing the missing metadata directly should keep the review focused
  on the checked-out diff instead of extra discovery turns

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

commit.message:
fix(review): harden GitHub review workflows

Summary:
- pass explicit PR context into the Claude review workflow prompt and
  forbid shell-based PR discovery in that automation path
- enable full Claude action output for debugging review failures
- read PR descriptions from the GitHub event payload in the lint job
  instead of interpolating raw body text into shell

Rationale:
- the automated review job was looping on denied gh commands because it
  was trying to rediscover PR metadata at runtime
- full action logs are needed to diagnose Claude review failures in
  GitHub Actions
- PR descriptions can contain apostrophes and backticks that break shell
  assignment if the body is injected directly

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml .github/workflows/pr-description-lint.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(review): enable full Claude action logs

Summary:
- enable full output for the GitHub Claude review workflow so failed
  runs expose the agent loop in Actions logs
- add a repository guard that requires show_full_output in the review
  workflow configuration

Rationale:
- the external review job is currently failing at runtime and needs
  full logs for diagnosis
- keeping the guard test in place prevents the debug visibility from
  silently regressing

Tests:
- npx vitest run test/repository/tooling.test.ts
- npx prettier --check .github/workflows/github-review.yml test/repository/tooling.test.ts

commit.message:
test(decision_api): add deep branch coverage for unhappy paths (issue #33) (#36)

Adds 13 new scenarios targeting previously uncovered decision_api.lua branches:
- rule_engine returns nil/invalid decision → 503
- throttle delay_ms > MAX_THROTTLE_DELAY_MS (30000) is clamped to 30s
- throttle delay_ms 0 → no sleep, access proceeds
- reject with no retry_after → Retry-After defaults to "1"
- LLM JSON error body for prompt_tokens_exceeded reason
- LLM JSON error body for max_tokens_per_request_exceeded reason
- JWT with 4+ segments (too many dots) → nil
- JWT with 1 segment (only one dot) → nil
- ip_country falls back to X-Country-Code header when geoip var absent
- empty host variable → nil host in request context
- allow decision with no headers proceeds without exiting
- log_handler status 200 → no event queued

547 unit + 65 integration tests passing, luacheck clean.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Avoid proxying clean nested objects

Skip the non-plain sanitizing proxy when a nested phase-two value does
not itself contain a deferred prompt placeholder.

Add a regression test that keeps a clean nested object with a
private-field-backed method identical and callable while a sibling
field still requires phase-two scrubbing.

commit.message:
Keep nested collection identity stable

Skip cloning nested array, set, and map values during deferred-
prompt scrubbing when that nested collection does not itself
contain a deferred placeholder.

Add a regression test that keeps a clean Set subclass identical and
unproxied while a sibling field still requires phase-two scrubbing.

commit.message:
Avoid mutating parent field states

Replace object() field-state propagation's in-place parent mutation
with a per-parent weak cache so inherited annotation state is
reused without modifying the caller's object.

Add a regression test that keeps the original child state object in
place while complete() still succeeds with inherited annotations.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(safety): add approval guardrails and safer fetches (#29)

* docs(design): add phase 3 TUI refinement design doc

Add design document for Phase 3 TUI refinement, focusing on:
- Content-first minimalist design philosophy
- Removal of Panel borders and visual clutter
- Refined color palette inspired by Claude Code
- Component specifications with before/after comparisons
- Migration plan from Phase 2 to cleaner terminal UI

* feat(tui): implement Phase 3 TUI refinement

Redesign the terminal UI to be more elegant and content-first:

- Replace Panel borders with plain text layout
- Add Theme class with refined color palette (DIM, SUCCESS, ERROR)
- Add STATUS_ICONS with spinner (⠋), checkmark (✓), and error (✗) indicators
- Simplify session header to single line with path truncation
- Refactor help display to use aligned columns with partition()
- Refactor status display to use batched console output
- Refactor history display with turn separators and limit parameter
- Update prompt symbol to amber-colored ›
- Remove unused rich imports (Panel, Rule, Table)
- Add docstrings to all public methods

Fixes from simplify review:
- Replace cryptic 'w' variable with 'label_width'
- Cache excess count to avoid double len() check in show_history()

All 66 tests pass.

* fix(tui): address Phase 3 TUI refinement review comments

- Wire custom amber › prompt indicator into REPL by making prompt_text
  optional in PromptInput protocol and calling prompt() without args
- Re-truncate turn text in /history output to 80 chars to keep it compact
- Remove duplicated newline hint from /help (already in command_help_lines)

* fix(cli): close TUI review regressions

Summary:
- align help rendering for all slash commands, including /history
- update the smoke prompt double to match the optional REPL prompt
  signature
- add a presenter regression test for consistent help formatting

Rationale:
- the TUI refinement left one malformed help row because rendering
  depended on spacing in the source strings
- the REPL prompt fix changed the protocol shape and broke the
  interactive smoke path until the fake was updated to match

Tests:
- PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run pytest -p no:cacheprovider
  tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py::test_run_chat_command_interactive_starts_fresh_session_each_time

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Increase default max_iterations from 8 to 80

* docs(design): align phase-3 tool policy docs with actual implementation

Update the design doc to reflect the shipped behavior where all tools
are enabled by default, with --deny-* flags to disable them:

- ToolPolicy defaults: allow_write=True, allow_bash=True (was False)
- CLI flags: --deny-write, --deny-bash, --deny-network (was --allow-*)
- Add policy resolution order documentation
- Update Write, Edit, Bash tool sections to reflect enable-by-default

The docs now match the actual code in policy.py, main.py, and tests.

* fix(cli): show enabled tools in status

Summary:
- thread enabled tool names from the tool registry into /status
- render a Tools line in the presenter and document the behavior
- add a CLI regression test for interactive status output

Rationale:
- Phase 3 docs call out the enabled tool set as recommended startup
- and status behavior, but the REPL only surfaced session metadata.
- Exposing registry-backed tool names keeps the CLI aligned with runtime
- policy without duplicating tool configuration state.

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
- broader run of tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py and
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py hit an unrelated existing failure in
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py:170

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(tools): harden default tool output

Summary:
- preserve structured tool summaries in the TUI and keep long token
  arguments identifiable
- make web_fetch byte-limited decoding UTF-8 safe and normalize ddgs
  source metadata
- expand filesystem and tool registry coverage and align phase-3 docs
  with the shipped behavior

Rationale:
- tool activity lines need readable identifiers for paths and URLs
- byte-aware decoding and non-ASCII result content avoid data loss at
  tool boundaries
- docs and tests should reflect the actual default tool behavior

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/unit/test_local_fs_tools.py tests/unit/test_web_tools.py
  tests/unit/test_tool_registry.py tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(safety): add approval guardrails and safer fetches

Summary:
- restore the default agent loop safety budget to 8
- add CLI and settings overrides for max_iterations and
  max_html_extract_bytes
- persist oversized web_fetch results as artifacts and fail safely when
  truncated HTML extraction cannot produce readable text
- require approval for write/edit, bash, and network tools with a session
  auto-approve path

Rationale:
- keep unsafe autonomy bounded until runtime approval guardrails exist
- borrow mature coding-agent patterns without copying their larger default
  loop budgets blindly
- avoid returning corrupted fetch output when large HTML pages are truncated

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
  tests/unit/test_tool_registry.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): validate max settings and prompt only on tty

Summary:
- reject non-positive max iteration and HTML extract byte values at
  the CLI boundary before persisting or running chat
- require both stdin and stdout to be TTYs before prompting for
  unsafe tool approvals in one-shot mode
- add regression tests for invalid init values and redirected stdout

Rationale:
- prevent `agentlet init` from writing settings that later fail to
  load during normal chat usage
- avoid leaking approval prompts into redirected output and hanging
  scripts that expect one-shot stdout capture

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_settings.py
  tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback for safety guardrails

- Fix truncated User-Agent string in web_fetch tool
- Add 10MB maximum bound for max_html_extract_bytes setting
- Document behavioral change: allow_write/allow_bash default to True
- Set restrictive permissions (0o600) on fetch artifact files

* fix(cli): preserve approvals and history labels

Summary:
- route one-shot approval prompts through stderr so stdout stays clean
- carry original turn numbers through history summaries and rendering
- add regression tests for redirected approvals and trimmed history output

Rationale:
- one-shot approvals should still work when command output is redirected
- history labels must match the real transcript after trimming recent turns

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): address Phase 3 code review feedback for safety guardrails

Issue 1: Allow approval prompts when stdin is redirected
- Add /dev/tty fallback in InteractiveApprovalHandler for piped input
- Add _tty_attempted flag to cache failed open attempts
- Add context manager support and close() method for resource cleanup
- Add isatty() validation on fallback TTY

Issue 2: Enforce max_html_extract_bytes upper bound in CLI
- Add MAX_HTML_EXTRACT_BYTES_LIMIT constant to policy.py
- Consolidate _int_at_least and _int_in_range into single function
- Apply range validation to both init and chat subcommands
- Update settings.py to use the shared constant

All 107 tests pass.

* fix(cli): convert REPL to async for proper approval handling

Convert run_repl() to async to fix asyncio.run() nesting error when
tool approval prompts are triggered during interactive sessions.

- Change run_repl() to async def and use await for loop.run_turn()
- Move asyncio.run() to entry point in chat_app.py
- Remove unused asyncio import from repl.py

This fixes the "asyncio.run() cannot be called from a running event loop"
error and ensures approval prompts are routed to the correct handler
instead of being captured as new REPL queries.

* fix(cli): use prompt_async for async REPL

Add prompt_async method to PromptInput protocol and use it in run_repl()
to properly support async execution with prompt_toolkit.

- Add prompt_async to PromptInput protocol in repl.py
- Use await prompt_input.prompt_async() instead of prompt()
- Update FakePromptSession in tests to implement prompt_async

This fixes the event loop conflict when running REPL in async mode.

* fix(cli): harden interactive approval prompts

Summary:
- use async approval prompts in the interactive REPL to avoid event-loop
  reentrancy failures
- close approval handlers in one-shot and interactive chat flows to
  release any fallback tty handles
- narrow approval-prompt EOF handling with a dedicated exception and add
  CLI regression coverage for approval, EOF, and provider failure paths

Rationale:
- prompt_toolkit approvals run inside an active event loop, so reusing the
  sync prompt path caused asyncio.run() failures during interactive turns
- approval prompt closure should end the session cleanly without masking
  unrelated EOF errors from providers or tools
- explicit lifecycle cleanup and focused regression tests keep the CLI
  approval flow stable as the TUI evolves

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py -q

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(agent-loop): stop re-running stale approved tool calls

Summary:
- ignore residual tool_calls on terminal assistant responses
- keep final assistant messages and session records free of stale calls
- add regression coverage for agent loop and interactive approval flow

Rationale:
- some streamed provider responses can end with finish_reason=stop
  while still carrying old tool_calls in the payload
- treating those stale calls as executable caused repeated approval
  prompts after the final answer and could loop indefinitely

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_agent_loop.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py -k "interactive_approval
  or stale_tool_calls or one_shot_approval_prompts"
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_litellm_provider.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* test(cli): harden interactive chat coverage

Summary:
- add direct run_repl tests for commands, interrupts, cancellation,
  and failure recovery
- add prompt_toolkit smoke coverage for multiline terminal input and
  interactive approval flow
- keep transcript assertions in the smoke path so interactive state is
  validated end to end

Rationale:
- interactive bugs were clustering around branches that fake provider
  tests hit only indirectly
- covering both REPL control flow and real prompt_toolkit input makes
  regressions in terminal-mode chat easier to catch early

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_repl.py
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py tests/unit/test_sessions.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): restore repl prompt after approval

Summary:
- restore the main PromptSession message after interactive approval prompts
- add regression tests for sticky approval prompts in the handler and chat flow

Rationale:
- prevent approval prompt text from persisting into the main REPL input
- stop yes/all replies from being misrouted as normal user messages

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_cli_approvals.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py -q
- uv run pytest tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py -q

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(readme): update to reflect actual implementation

- Add missing settings: max_iterations, max_html_extract_bytes, tool policies
- Document all CLI options including --auto-approve and --deny-* flags
- Add new provider examples (Cohere, Mistral, Fireworks)
- Update project structure with all source files
- Add "Built-in Tools" section documenting all 8 tools
- Fix keyboard shortcuts to match actual behavior
- Add security notes about file permissions and legacy migration

commit.message:
feat: replace ChromaDB with Qdrant for vector storage

- Replace chromadb dependency with qdrant-client>=1.10.1
- Add Qdrant service to docker-compose.yml (ports 6333/6334)
- Create synapse/storage/qdrant_client.py wrapper with:
  - Thai text preprocessing before indexing
  - Payload filtering support
  - Lazy embedder loading
  - Mock embedding for development
- Update semantic.py, episodic.py, procedural.py layers
- Update storage/__init__.py to export QdrantClient
- Update graphiti_mcp_server.py for Qdrant initialization

Decision: Mneme + Codex consensus (2026-03-15)
- Qdrant chosen for explicit Thai support (Charabia tokenizer)
- Official MCP server available
- Better payload filtering for Five-Layer Memory

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-Authored-By: Mneme <mneme@cerberus.ai>

commit.message:
chore(workflow): add Symphony setup and launcher

Summary:
- add a repo-specific Symphony workflow template under Symphony/WORKFLOW.md
- add scripts/run_symphony.sh to bootstrap/update openai/symphony and run it
- support local gitignored config via .symphony.local.env and Keychain API-key fallback
- document setup and run flow in README and docs/SYMPHONY_SETUP.md

Rationale:
- enable unattended Linear-driven work orchestration without introducing runtime
  dependencies into app modules
- keep secrets out of git while making the run command simple and reproducible

Tests:
- ./scripts/check_repo.sh
- ./scripts/test.sh
- ./scripts/run_extractor_stub.sh
- ./scripts/run_symphony.sh --port 4000 (manual startup verification)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Keep primitive prompt state shapes intact

Avoid injecting annotation wrapper objects into primitive prompt
states before calling the inner parser, so parsers continue to see
the TState shape they declared.

Keep annotation-backed wrapper states working through prompt.complete()
and add regressions for primitive state shape, optional wrapper
fallback, and concurrent non-plain parser reuse.

commit.message:
Reuse scrubbed phase-two parsed values

Prepare scrubbed phase-two parsed values once per annotation-
collection pass so every dynamic context in that pass sees the
same object identity.

This preserves WeakMap and strict-identity coordination even when
deferred prompt placeholders require sanitization, and adds a
regression test that shares scrubbed parsed metadata from a
dynamic context into a config loader.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): add reconnect flow for streamed RAG runs

Summary:
- parse SSE event ids in the web client and send `run_id` plus
  `Last-Event-ID` when reconnecting a streamed RAG response
- persist a minimal resumable run snapshot, restore the interrupted
  transcript on reload, and surface an explicit reconnect banner
- restart the stream when replay is unavailable while deduplicating
  assistant text, sources, trace markers, and action cards for the run

Rationale:
- interrupted web reloads were silently losing the active stream state
  and nudging users toward a duplicate transcript
- the backend now supports replay by event id, so the client needs to
  preserve enough run metadata to reconnect safely
- fallback restart handling keeps the v1 flow resilient even when exact
  replay history is unavailable

Tests:
- `cd apps/axiom-web && pnpm lint`
- manual smoke: restored interrupted run banner, replay attempt with
  `Last-Event-ID: 4`, fallback restart without the header, and a single
  deduplicated `Alpha Beta Gamma` transcript with one source chip

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Scrub collection own properties in loaders

Teach config-loader deferred-prompt sanitization to inspect and
scrub extra own properties on array, set, and map parser results.

This keeps config callbacks from observing DeferredPromptValue in
collection subclasses that carry additional fields, and adds a
regression test for a Set subclass with an own apiKey property.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Preserve config loader parsed identity

Avoid cloning array, set, and plain-object parsed values before
calling config loaders when there is no deferred prompt placeholder
to scrub.

This keeps phase-two loader inputs identity-stable for ordinary
results and adds a regression test that shares parsed-object
metadata through a WeakMap across dynamic contexts and config
loading.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Allow wrapped primitive completion retries

Let prompt.complete() retry inner completion for annotation-backed
primitive wrapper states, while still treating prompt sentinel
states as interactive fallbacks.

This preserves annotation-driven parsers that materialize values in
complete() from injected wrapper state and adds a regression test
covering that path.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix toplevel function mode and unify special parser tests

- make --toplevel-function drive module codegen in function mode so top-level
  locals use fast locals/cells and nested scopes capture them correctly
- replace separate compile_only, optimize, and interactive test logic with
  manifest-driven tests under test/options/
- move scanner-only fixtures into test/scanner_only/ and drop unused legacy
  text fixtures

commit.message:
Keep prompt fallback for optional states

Restrict prompt.complete()'s annotation-aware inner completion
retry so pure annotation wrappers from optional-style no-input
states still fall through to the interactive prompt.

Keep source-backed states and non-plain parser states eligible for
annotation-aware complete() retries, and add a regression test for
prompt(optional(...)) under annotations.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Keep phase-two object identity stable

Avoid cloning plain phase-two parsed values when no deferred prompt
placeholder needs to be scrubbed.

This keeps dynamic contexts observing the original parsed object
identity for ordinary results while still sanitizing top-level and
nested deferred prompt values when they are present. Add a
regression test that checks shared identity across phase-two
contexts.

commit.message:
Hide top-level deferred context inputs

Treat a top-level deferred prompt placeholder as undefined before
phase-two context hooks run so non-config contexts do not observe
the internal sentinel object.

Add a regression test for runWith(prompt(bindConfig(...))) with an
additional dynamic context to keep that top-level path sanitized.

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
